entry.title: Is there any recommendation for using @cds.persistence.journal?
entry.content:
I’m looking for guidance on when (or if) it's recommended to use @CDS.persistence.journal.
From what I understand, a potential drawback is that it might not play well with multitenancy, when tenants want to use custom extensions.

 

If this is not the case, should this annotation be used as default, since it seems very advantageous.
Or are there any other drawbacks that I'm not aware of?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA direct connectivity with Event Mesh in Integration Suite
entry.content: We have resolved this issue. In SMICM we found that it is looking for Issuer/Root certificate ISRG-X1 and R10 which was downloaded from amqp host url https://<your-account>.eu10.a.eventmesh.integration.cloud.sap and added it in SSL Standard along with Anonymous.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition created from Service Order
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, when a service order with multiple external items is released, purchase requisitions are created for each service item. However, there is no standard configuration available Purchase Requisition for service order with external items to change the document type and number range specifically for these purchase requisitions derived from service orders with external items. This behavior is part of the expected functionality of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and currently, there isn’t a direct way to modify the document type or number range specifically for these purchase requisitions.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Map format data field FPAYHX-REF01
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

 

FPAYHX is a payment medium structure used in SAP's payment formats (e.g., SEPA, ACH, wire transfers).
 
The field REF01 usually stores reference information, such as invoice numbers, payment reference IDs, or customer/vendor identifiers.
 
I hope this will help you answer your question.
 
Best regards,
Jeremy

entry.title: Re: standard filters not working in internal career portal
entry.content:
@user116 wrote:

Hi,

Please, standard filters (business unit and division) are not working in the internal career portal even if we've activated the business unit object in the internal career search settings. Any help please?

Hello @user116,
Ensure the following steps are completed: e-zpasspa com

  1. Search Settings: Verify that the business unit and division filters are correctly enabled in the internal career search settings.
  2. Picklist Configuration: Confirm that the picklists for these filters are properly configured and mapped.
  3. Role Permissions: Check if the necessary permissions are granted for users to access these filters.
  4. Cache Refresh: Clear the system cache or perform a data refresh to apply changes.

Best Regards,
James Carty

entry.title: Re: In S/4HANA Public Cloud why do we select a period if...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking the questions in the SAP community.

Here’s some answers that you can take into consideration :

 

  1. Why do they need to select a period if they want to do consolidation for the full fiscal year?

In SAP Group Reporting, the period selection is required because consolidation happens at a specific time period level, even if the goal is to consolidate for the full fiscal year.

Period 12 (as shown in the screenshot) represents the final period of the fiscal year, meaning all data up to that point is considered.

SAP requires period selection for each run, even if the full fiscal year is included. This ensures proper periodic closing and adjustments are captured accurately.

 

  1. Why isn’t a transport request created when saving this configuration?

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, certain configuration settings are client-specific and stored locally, not requiring a transport request.

The Set Global Parameters configuration in Group Reporting is typically a business configuration setting, meaning it is maintained directly in the system without being transported across tenants.

Unlike On-Premise SAP, where configuration is moved using transports, SAP Cloud Public Edition relies on Central Business Configuration (CBC) or other cloud-based mechanisms for managing settings across environments.

 

I hope those elements will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: BW Bridge: In what schema can I find table TFACS?
entry.content:
Hi,

do you mean for a solution within BW Bridge or in SAP Datasphere?

In Datasphere, the approach is documented in the blog I shared, where you can join the Datasphere view SAP.TIME.M_TIME_DIMENSION with the S/4HANA view I_FACTORYCALWORKINGDAYSPERYR to get the desired working day calculations.

In BW Bridge it's a bit different. You do have access to an ABAP API, but this would mean stepping outside of SQL/AMDP logic. You can use the following API:

  • Interface: IF_FHC_FCAL_RUNTIME

  • Method: CALC_WORKINGDAYS_BETWEEN_DATES
    More details can be found here:
    👉https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/rel-comp-factory-calendar#reading-factory-calendar-related-information

If you're looking for a pure SQL-based solution in BW Bridge using AMDP, there currently isn’t a released CDS view equivalent to I_FACTORYCALWORKINGDAYSPERYR from S/4HANA. You’d likely need to build your own logic by joining views like I_FactoryCalendarBasic with standard date views such as I_CalendarDate.

You might also find some inspiration or examples in this SAP Community thread:
👉https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/wanted-i-calendardate-like-abap-cds-view-for-work-days-of-a-factory/qaq-p/12532183

If it's an option for your use case, I would still recommend handling this logic in Datasphere, as it's much more flexible for these types of calculations.

Best regards,
David

entry.title: Shape the Future of the Next-Generation Inbox
entry.content:
Attention My Inbox Users! 

Welcome to the future of task management with SAP Task Center, the next-generation inbox designed to streamline your workflow and keep you organized. If you use My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your input is vital for making SAP Task Center truly exceptional. 

What is SAP Task Center? 

SAP Task Center is a unified inbox for task management across multiple SAP and non-SAP solutions. It aims to provide a centralized hub where you can manage all your tasks efficiently, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. 

Why Your Feedback Matters 

As a user of My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your feedback is crucial for our development process. By sharing your experiences - the good, the bad, and everything in between - you can directly shape the evolution of SAP Task Center. This collaborative effort ensures that the next-generation inbox is tailored to your specific needs and workflows.

How You Can Help

Participate in our survey and let us know what you think about My Inbox. Your insights will help us build a tool that truly understands and meets your workflow requirements.

Survey: Help Shape the Future of SAP Task Center

Together, we can make SAP Task Center an inbox that not only meets but exceeds your expectations. Your time and contributions are greatly appreciated.

If you’re curious to learn more about SAP Task Center, check out the SAP Task Center Product Page.

Thank you for being a part of this journey. Let’s build something remarkable together!

entry.content:
Hi @Vera_Jiang 

We have the following systems migrated to S/4 BW HANA and to private cloud "RISE with SAP". EB2, QB2, PB2.

Now we have both old and new entries for the same SID's in SLD and LMDB.

EB2, QB2, PB2 (old entries) EB200001, QB200001, PB200001 (There is complete changes in Hostname, Installation numbers and system numbers)

As per KBA 1727294 will already have the recommended value of 1 is used but still duplicated got created.

We have the following applications Charm, ITSM are configured for the old system entries EB2, QB2, PB2.

In order to Reuse existing system identifiers entries for Change Request Management application data without having to create new configurations for the migrated system from scratch for the migrated systems EB200001, QB200001, PB200001 and there are open change documents.

I have the went through the following 

KBA's 1694004 - Dealing with duplicate technical system names (SIDs)

KBA 1747926 - Dealing with duplicate technical system names (old SLD)

section 3.3 Reuse Existing System Identifier Entries in Change Request Management) from SAP Document link Migration of a Customer-Managed System to Service Provider Landscape (Private Cloud)

Kindy provide some guidance for right approach from the mentioned "KBAs or shared link 3.3 Reuse Existing System Identifier Entries in Change Request Management) to Reuse existing system identifiers entries for Change Request Management application data without having to create new configurations for the migrated system from scratch and also without data loss as we have open change.

Regards

Srinivas V

entry.title: Has Joule cross business-role data bleeding been addressed, and how?
entry.content:
With the case of Joule being embedded throughout the core of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public ecosystem, it's natural to assume that Joule has access to all the data in the underlying datastores - which makes sense. However, as with any LLM there substantial considerations regarding bleeding data, either directly or as derivatives. I'm not worried about out-of-organisation security issues, more so cross-organisation security complications.

 

I have been looking around for articles or blog posts regarding Joule's grasp on this / measures in place to prevent bleeding, but I haven't found any.

In the most simple case, imagine two business roles;

- Junior Procurement/Sourcing 

- Senior Procurement/Sourcing

 

The only difference is the junior does not have access to supplier contact information to protect business IP. Given both roles have access to Joule, I would expect outputs of Joule to be governed by similarly to defined access rights in the individual Business Role Access Categories.

In this case, access rights to specific fields are relatively simple, and could essentially be dealt with in the same way as LLM's deal with copyright material / LORA's etc. However, this becomes increasingly complex, and is by no means trivial, as the amount of business roles, data access, data and output types increase.

So:

1. How - technically - does Joule enforce the same minimal-access principles as there exists around RBAC? 

2. Does there exist generalized analytics/monitoring around Joule interactions to identify and act upon data-bleeding instances?

3. Does Joule always work across tenant-data, or is "Joule" an individual agent per. user/union of unique business role combinations scoped to only have access to the data a business role(s) has access to, and is it actively designed to prevent access to more data in the context window?

Unless there exists a formal article on the technical implementation of this, which we can refer to with regards to data sensitivity, it must be assumed that data bleeding is at best possible and at worst not seriously considered in Joule.

entry.title: New Reporting Permission for Workflow Process Request Data for People Analytics Story Reporting
entry.content:
The permission settings that are provide for managing workflow requests are optimized for the workflow request management. The existing permissions related to the Workflow processing are dedicated to very specific activities and are not suited to be evaluated in reporting. In particular many customers are hesitating to provide the permission for “Manage Workflow Requests” to all report users. Starting from 1H 2505 a new reporting permission “Report Workflow Process Details” was introduced which is evaluated in story reports dealing with workflow requests. This blogpost highlights the reasons why this new permission was created.

Introduction

In an ideal case the setup of the workflows works fully automated, i.e. workflow requests are created for various kind of changes such as e.g. leave requests, salary changes, promotions, hires, transfers, terminations and many more. As soon as a particular change is done, a workflow request is created dependent of the workflow configuration which appears in the ToDo-List of the respective processor; typically managers, cost centre responsible managers, HR administrators and others.

Only in exceptional cases it is required that a very limited group of administrators checks the workflow requests and might trigger respective changes (e.g. changes the approver since the configured approver is on a longer absence). The cockpit for such activities is the Admin Center activity “Manage Workflow Requests”. Figure 1 shows this activity with the search functionality (shown in red) and the potential actions that can be triggered (shown in yellow), as e.g. changing the approver or adding other approvers. With access to “Manage Workflow Requests” it is possible (1) to see the sensitive content of a workflow (even if a user does not have access e.g. to the sensitive compensation details) and (2) to make changes into the workflow process, such as e.g. approving, declining, withdrawing and commenting workflow requests.

Before H1 2505 “Manage Workflow Requests” was evaluated for workflow reporting in People Analytics. However, workflow request related reports are typically rolled out for a much larger group of report users compared to the smaller audience of HR administrators which monitor “Manage Workflow Requests”. Therefore, starting from H1 2505 a new reporting permission “Report Workflow Process Details” was introduced which is evaluated in story reports dealing with workflow requests.

Figure 1   Admin Center activity “Manage Workflow Requests”

Figure 2   Example of a Workflow Requests

Solution Approach

Figure 3 shows this new permission setting “Report Workflow Process Details”. Only if this permission is granted, then Workflow Requests details can be reported. To prevent disruptions to existing story reports with workflow request data, the new permission “Report Workflow Process Details” is automatically granted to those roles which have already the permission for "Manage Workflow Requests".

Figure 3   Role Based Permission settings under “Manage Workflows”

Also note that reports based on MDF objects which should report the respective workflow details require the permission “Report Workflow Process Details” so that the workflow details are accordingly filled. Figure 4 shows such a use case as example; with such a report it is possible to report on both, the approved record (where Position-mdfRecordStatus is filled with “N”) together with the pending record (Position-mdfRecordStatus = “P”) while the pending record will provide the respective workflow details as long as permission for “Report Workflow Process Details” is granted (if the permission for “Report Workflow Process Details” is not given, the workflow fields will remain empty).

In this context it is also worth to mention that starting from 1H 2025 a join was implemented from custom MDF objects to “Generic Object Workflow Request” which allow to create queries based on the custom MDF object with the respective workflow details. Prerequisite is that the attribute “pending data” is set to "yes" on Admin Center -> Configure Object Definition (note that for 'standard' MDF objects as e.g. 'Position' as shown in Figure 4 the join was already generated before 1H 2025). This enhancement allows to track e.g. who approved a request and when this was done as well as for pending workflow requests to track who is responsible for approval.

Figure 4   Example of a query which provides MDF details together with respective workflow details

entry.title: Re: Saving / opening a model (PDM) takes far too long
entry.content:
There have been reports of issues like this for a long time. One of the previous product managers told me that the number of diagrams can be an issue when opening a model, as PD loads every diagram into memory when you open a model; perhaps the number of symbols on those diagrams is also relevant?

Is there anything, apart from size, that distinguishes this model from your other PDMs? Does it contain shortcuts to or replications from other models?

I have an email trail from the previous SAP Community about PDM performance in 2015; it's not available anymore, but here's what it says.

Question

are there any options to speed up PowerDesigner? I have a big PDM with more than 600 tables (with references, sequences) and growing. My Workstation is a Lenovo T420 with 8GB RAM and CPU is Intel Core i5-2450M 2,5GHz.

Answer

There is no "speed-up" option in the product (or it would be set, read-only, and hidden). But you can de-activate some fancy options, like Links options in Model options / General settings. You can also create several diagrams in your model and display the tables/references separated by concept. In the same spirit, you can divide the whole model in many smaller models and use shortcuts for references

entry.title: Re: Is there any recommendation for using @cds.persistenc...
entry.content:
Hi Alexander,

It is better to use @CDS.persistence.journal only to critical tables where maintaining a history of changes is truly needed.

If this annotation is used by default on an entity, every update or delete (yes, even delete— it creates a new journal entry marking the deletion) saves a new version of the record instead of changing/deleting the existing one.

This means the database size will keep growing over time.

Now please consider the read part. With @CDS.persistence.journal enabled, fetching the latest record requires filtering out historical versions: -

SELECT * FROM <table> WHERE <col_name> = '<val>' ORDER BY modifiedAt DESC LIMIT 1;

If the table contains millions of records, this query could take a significant amount of time to execute.

A good read on the same here: -

CAP Enhancements to Know 

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Custom dimension in FTE measure
entry.content:
Hello!

Our client has requested to add an additional custom dimension to be used with the FTE measure. In the FTE measure, they want to include employees which are Active and on Unpaid Leave (Leave of Absence), however, they would like to be able to filter not only based on employment status, but on the event as well (or a combination of both). They are requesting that because they have multiple Leave of Absence event reasons, and some of them are associated with status "Unpaid Leave" and others with "Active". Basically, in their FTE measure they would like to see which employees are with status Unpaid Leave, which ones are Active but on Leave of Absence and which are regular Active employees. We were thinking of adding both A and U status in the FTE measure as the condition, and creating a calculated column which combines the Event and the Employment Status from Job Information. Based on this calculated column, we would like to create a custom dimension for Employment Status with the following logic: 

  • If (Status is Active+ Event = Leave of Absence, then custom status will be Active LOA)
  • If (Status is  Active+ Event is not equal to Leave of Absence, then custom status will be Active)
  • If (Status is Unpaid Leave + Event= Leave of Absence, then custom status is Unpaid Leave). 

    We will try to test this as well, but from your experience, do you think it would work? Have you had any similar requests to this? Thank you!

entry.title: New: All 3 Process Flows available by default in SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition!
entry.content:
What’s new?

Customers can now request a comprehensive analysis with the latest version of SAP Notes, and they will always get all three Process Flows (and hence don't need to choose anymore).

Which 3 Process Flows are available?

  • Supplier invoice issuing (with purchase order) to FI-AP clearing (KPPURCH460): Measures the number of supplier invoices created in reference to a purchase order in the reference period and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took. Learn More.
  • Sales billing document creation to FI-AR clearing (KPSD000260): Measures the number of sales billing documents created in the reference period for which a corresponding FI document and customer payment are expected and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took. Lean More.
  • Purchase order item creation (with account assignment) to invoice receipt creation (KPPURCH268): Measures the number of purchase order items created in the reference period for which a goods receipt as well as an invoice receipt are expected and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took.​ Learn More.

How to extract the data and request Discovery Edition?

The instructions in the How-to Guide supports customers through the process.

Customers can get all information and request their free SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition via our landing page: https://url.sap/discovery-edition

entry.title: Zertifizierung für SAP-Partner: Die Neuerungen im Überblick - inklusive Q&A
entry.content:
Am 19. März luden Suzanne Heiob, SAP Certification Product Management, und Axel Meinke, Partner Enablement Manager MEE, SAP-Partner ein, um über die neuesten Updates im SAP-Zertifizierungsprogramm zu informieren. Die Session bot Einblicke in die Transformation des Zertifizierungsprogramms und stellte eine Reihe von Updates vor. In der Session wurde auch Raum für Fragen und Diskussion geboten. Damit Ihr Euch als Partner auf die neuen Anforderungen vorbereiten könnt, findet Ihr hier eine fokussierte Zusammenfassung der Webinar-Inhalte sowie zugehörige Fragen aus dem Q&A.

 

Was ist neu im SAP-Zertifizierungsprogramm?

Eine zentrale Änderung im Zertifizierungsprogramm ist der Fokus auf rollenbasierte Zertifizierungen. Ziel ist es, die Fähigkeiten von SAP-Partnern präzise auf praktische Anforderungen abzustimmen. Die neuen Zertifizierungen decken kritische Rollen ab, wie etwa Enterprise Architects und Implementation Consultants, die in der Vergangenheit aufgrund des starken Produkt Fokus nicht ausreichend repräsentiert waren. Zudem wurden nicht mehr relevante Zertifizierungen aus dem Portfolio entfernt, um den Fokus auf die Unterstützung bei Cloud-Einführungen und S/4HANA-Transformationen zu legen.

In diesem Blogpost findet Ihr weitere Details zu den Neuerungen des letzten Jahres sowie Beispiele für rollenbasierte Zertifizierungen.

Weitere Zertifizierungsanforderungen für SAP-Partner  

Bis Juli 2025 müssen alle Partnerressourcen in Schlüsselrollen – Unternehmensarchitekten, Entwickler, Berater für Geschäftstransformationsmanagement, Projektmanager, die an RISE with SAP-Projekten beteiligt sind – über eine gültige SAP-Zertifizierung verfügen. Bis Ende 2025 wird erwartet, dass 50% der relevanten Partner-Praktiker mindestens eine gültige Zertifizierung im SAP-Lösungsportfolio besitzen. Insgesamt bedeutet dies für SAP-Partner, dass sie ihre Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich aktualisieren und erweitern müssen. Mehr Informationen dazu auch im SAP News Artikel ‚Stets auf dem neuesten Stand bei Skills: SAP und Partner verpflichten sich zur Kompetenzvalidierung‘.

Das ‚Get- and Stay-Certified‘ Modell

Ab April 2024 müssen Zertifizierungen jährlich erneuert werden, was durch ein sogenanntes ‚Stay-Certified‘-Assessment erfolgt. Dieses kurze Quiz erfordert in der Regel bis zu zwei Stunden Lernaufwand, dauert 20-30 Minuten und verlängert die Zertifizierung um jeweils 12 Monate. Dies ist besonders wichtig für alle, die ihre Zertifizierungen auch nach dem 31. März 2025 behalten möchten, da ohne das Ablegen eines solchen Assessments der Zertifizierungsstatus verfällt.

Das kontinuierliche Nachweisen der Kenntnisse stellt sicher, dass Expert:innen immer auf dem neuesten Stand der SAP-Innovationen sind. Das sichert zum einen die Qualität von SAP-Projekten und zum anderen individuelle Wettbewerbsfähigkeit auf dem Arbeitsmarkt. Um das sichtbar machen zu können, gibt es sowohl für das Ablegen von Zertifizierungen als auch für das Abschließen von Learning Journeys (inklusive eines kurzen Selbsttests) digitale Badges. Diese können beispielsweise auf LinkedIn geteilt oder auf Credly eingesehen werden.

SAP Universal ID: Einfacher Zertifizierungstransfer

Eine wichtige Neuerung ist die Einführung der SAP Universal ID. Diese ID ist ein lebenslang gültiger persönlicher Identifikator, der dazu beiträgt, Zertifizierungen über unterschiedliche Arbeitgebende hinweg zu verwalten. Um die Universal ID zu aktivieren, müssen mindestens zwei verschiedene E-Mail-Adressen, darunter eine private, verknüpft sein. Dies stellt sicher, dass der Zugang zur ID auch bei einem Wechsel des Unternehmens erhalten bleibt.

SAP Learning Hub und Zertifizierungen

Ein Abonnement für SAP Learning Hub umfasst den Zugriff auf alle Lernmaterialien der SAP sowie vier Prüfungsversuche für Zertifizierungen pro Jahr. Die Kosten für die initiale Zertifizierung und die ‚Stay Certified‘-Assessments sind also in der SAP Learning Hub Subskription enthalten. Eine Zertifizierung bleibt 12 Monate gültig, solange die regelmäßigen Assessments bestanden werden. Die Re-Zertifizierung benötigt dabei keinen der Zertifizierungsvoucher mehr und kann unbeschränkt oft versucht werden.

Besonders für die Prüfungsvorbereitung sind Live-Sessions in SAP Learning Hub nützlich, z.B. die Formate ‚Structured Guided Learning‘ oder ‚Stay Certified‘. Darüber hinaus erhaltet Ihr Zugang zu vorkonfigurierten Schulungssystemen, um Praxisübungen durchzuführen. Mit einem Abonnement für SAP Learning Hub seid Ihr also bestens aufgestellt: Ihr habt die Flexibilität, Eure Lernzeiten individuell zu gestalten und könnt Euch auf Prüfungen vorbereiten sowie diese ablegen. Diese Kombination aus eigenständigem Lernen, praktischen Übungsmöglichkeiten in Testumgebungen und interaktiven Sessions stellt sicher, dass Ihr optimal auf Eure Zertifizierungen vorbereitet seid.

Das Learning Hub Adoption Dashboard ist zudem ein wertvolles Tool, das es Learning Managern erlaubt, die Zertifizierungstransformation im Unternehmen zu monitoren und zu steuern. Hier könnt Ihr relevante Informationen zum Lern- und Zertifizierungsstatus Eurer Mitarbeitenden einsehen und notwendige Schritte zur Sicherung des Zertifizierungsstatus bis zur Deadline koordinieren.

Q&A aus der Session

Universal ID

 

Ich habe eine Frage zur Universal ID. Wieso muss ich 2 E-Mail-Adressen hinterlegen? Und wieso müssen die 2 E-Mail-Adressen verschiedene Domänen haben? Das resultiert ja darin, dass jeder der eine Universal SAP ID benötigt, eine private E-Mail-Adresse hinterlegen muss.

Der Zweck der zweiten E-Mail ist der Recovery Fall, falls Sie die Zugangsdaten verlieren. Es ist auch richtig, dass sich eine private E-Mail als Recovery empfiehlt, den die UID begleitet sie ein Karriereleben lang (im Gegensatz zum alten Userkonzept, in dem S-User z.B. ihrem Arbeitgeber "gehörten".

Bei der Fehlermeldung: "Can't see your certifications? Your emails linked to SAP Universal ID sind nicht verified. Wie verifiziert man seine Mail Adresse, wenn diese bereits seit Jahren die zugehörige Mailadresse ist?

Sie müssten auf die entsprechende E-mailadresse eine Verifizierungsmail erhalten haben. Falls Sie nicht da ist, prüfen Sie ggf. mal den Spam Ordner.

Zertifizierungen

 

Wo finde ich eine Übersicht, welche Zertifizierungen in meiner Firma schon existieren?     

Sofern Sie Learning Manager sind, können Sie sich per Ticket die Reporter Rolle für das Learning Hub Dashboard zuweisen lassen. Dort finden Sie unter Certifications die Übersicht Ihrer zertifizierten Berater (Voraussetzung ist, dass die Lerner auch eine Lizenz für SAP Learning Hub haben).

Ich habe Zertifizierungen unter unterschiedlichen S-User/ C-User gemacht. Was muss ich tun, um diese Zertifizierung zur neuen UID zu transformieren?         

Sie müssen die UID generieren und die zertifizierten S-/-P-User hinzufügen (simples Kontextmenü - "Add User" im Prozess).

Zählen Zertifizierungen, die man ohne LH erlangt, nicht für die Firma?        

Doch - man kann ja Zertifizierungsversuche auch direkt kaufen. Re-Zertifizierung geht nur über den SAP LH.

Gibt es eine Übersicht über die Zertifizierungen auch ohne Learning Hub-Zugang?

Eine Übersicht über alle Zertifizierungen ist hier zu finden: https://learning.sap.com/certifications?page=1. Die Übersicht über Ihre zertifizierten Lerner - unabhängig von Lizenzen für SAP Learning Hub - finden Sie auch in SAP for Me.

Kann eine 2022 absolvierte Zertifizierung die auf eine alte Universal ID (alter Arbeitsgeber) läuft auf eine neue übertragen werden & wenn ja, wie?   

Die UID gehört nicht wie der User im alten Konzept einem Arbeitgeber, sondern Ihnen persönlich. Sie müssen in Ihrem Fall lediglich die S-User ID Ihres neuen Arbeitgebers zu Ihrer bestehenden UID hinzufügen.

Es wurde in den letzten Wochen kommuniziert, dass an SAP-Partner besondere Anforderungen bzgl. Zertifizierungen gestellt werden. Welche (Art) von Zertifizierungen werden denn konkret benötigt? Gibt es Must have Zertifizierungen?

Für Partner sind besonders die Zertifizierungen relevant, die im Partner Competency Framework gelistet sind: SAP Partner Portal | Competency and specialization.

Was bedeutet der Status zertifiziert bleiben "Abgeschlossen" und "in Bearbeitung"?

Den Status "abgeschlossen" gibt es nicht. Wir haben auf SAP Learning den Status "up to date" wenn jemand mit seiner aktuellen Zertifizierung valide ist und das due date noch lange hin. Dann gibt es "Stay certified due“, wenn das due date in den nächsten 2 Wochen liegt.

Aktuell besteht noch die Möglichkeit, Zertifizierungs-Versuche ohne LH separat zu kaufen. Ende letzten Jahres wurde seitens SAP häufig kommuniziert, dass eine Lizenz für SAP Learning Hub künftig verpflichtend sein wird und man ohne Learning Hub mittelfristig keine Zertifizierung mehr erwerben kann. Gibt es hierzu eine klare, eindeutige Aussage seitens SAP? Kann man weiterhin separat Zertifizierungsversuche erwerben ohne Lizenz für SAP Learning Hub?

"Stay certified": hierzu ist eine gültige SAP Learning Hub Subskription notwendig.

"Get certified" ist weiterhin auch ohne SAP Learning Hub Lizenz möglich. Z.B. in SAP Classroom Trainings / Academies sofern CER enthalten ist, optional über vorhanden Certification Voucher.

 

"Stay Certified": hierzu ist eine gültige SAP Learning Hub Subskription notwendig.

Es gibt ein separates Paket mit 2 Zertifizierungsversuchen standalone. Diese Versuche können nur für die initiale Zertifizierung verwendet werden. Deltas zur Verlängerung der Validität müssen in der Folge über den LH abgelegt werden, daher ist der LH langfristig notwendig, um zertifiziert zu bleiben.

 

FYI hier auch der Link zum 2 Attempt: https://www.sap.com/germany/training-certification/sap-certification.html (Paket für 2 Zertifizierungsversuche).

Gibt es eine Übersicht, in der aufgelistet ist, welche Zertifizierungen wie viele Punkte für den Partnerstatus bringen? Woher wissen wir, welche wegfallen und welche Zertifizierungen bestehen blieben?

Das Partner Competency Framework ist hier: SAP Partner Portal | Competency and specialization.

Die Liste aller Zertifizierungen findet man hier: Browse SAP Certifications. Alle Zertifizierungen, die dort nicht gelistet sind, sind bereits retired.

Wenn die Prüfung bzgl. Re-Zertifizierung bis 31.3.25 nicht erfolgt, dann kann doch auch später die Prüfung abgelegt werden, oder?

Ja, aber man verliert seinen Status und ist dann nicht mehr zertifiziert, solange man die neue Prüfung noch nicht abgelegt hat. Und statt einfach nur den kürzeren Re-Zertifizierungstest zu machen, muss man sich dann komplett neu zertifizieren.

Der News Alert sagt aus "Bis Juli dieses Jahres müssen alle Partnerressourcen in Schlüsselpositionen – wie Unternehmensarchitekten, Entwickler, Berater für Business Transformationsmanagement und Projektmanager, die an Projekten von RISE with SAP beteiligt sind – über eine gültige SAP-Zertifizierung verfügen." - welche Zertifizierungen sind hier speziell gemeint?

Unter folgendem Link finden Sie eine Übersicht der notwendigen Trainings und Zertifizierungen für GROW und RISE: https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/competency/requirements.html

Findet man den Hinweis auf die Competency Relevance eines Zertifikats nur im Learning Hub Adoption Dashboard oder gibt es diese Übersicht auch außerhalb dieses Dashboards?

Das Partner Competency Framework findet man auch hier: SAP Partner Portal | Competency and specialization

Können wir auch Zertifizierungen unserer externen Berater "verwenden"? Auch wenn diese nicht bei uns angestellt sind?         

Sobald der externe Berater von ihrer Firma einen S-User bekommt und diesen S-User an seine SAP Universal ID verlinkt wird diese Zertifizierung auch für ihre Firma mitgezählt.

Wie wird der Zertifizierungsstatus der Berater in den Projekten geprüft?    

Über den Digital Badge. Dieser zeigt an, ob ein Berater noch gültig zertifiziert ist.

Was bedeutet der Status "Abgeschlossen" im SAP for me?    

Jemand hat das stay certified assessment gemacht und damit seine Zertifizierung verlängert.

Was bedeutet bei "Stay certified" = "in Progress"?     

Das bedeutet, dass der Lerner bereits eine Universal ID angelegt hat, aber noch kein Assessment bestanden hat.

Gibt es neue bzw. weitere Zertifizierungen für Berechtigungsberater?         

Aktuell ist hier keine neue Zertifizierung geplant. Anbei das Portal zur Prüfung, ob es für eine jeweilige Zertifizierung Nachfolger gibt.  SAP-Zertifizierungsfinder

Learning Manager

 

Wie werde ich Learning Manager bzw. wie kann ich weitere benennen

Initial im Vertrag – für weitere Benennungen sind hier Informationen https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/training/certified.html

Welche Ressourcen gibt es für Learning Manager

Im Learning Manager   Hub im Partner Portal gibt es Q&A, Email Templates und weitere hilfreiche Ressourcen.

SAP Learning Hub

 

Wenn ich über das Web in den Learning-Hub einloggen möchte, bekomme ich die Message „HTTP Status 400 - Bad Request“ Gibt es hier einen neuen Link? (https://learninghub.sap.com/login)

https://learninghub.sap.com ist die offizielle Seite für den SAP Learning Hub. Ggf. können Probleme auch am Browser liegen. In dem Fall bitte einen anderen ausprobieren.

Ich möchte wissen, wie ich überhaupt die Berechtigung "Learning Manager" bekomme?     

Die "IT Contact Person" erhält automatisch Zugang zum Learning Analytics Dashboard für alle Lernenden. Beim Kauf können Sie für weitere User eine Admin-Berechtigung beantragen per Ticket.

Learning.sap.com

 

Warum gibt es jetzt 2 Seiten: Learning.sap.com und Learninghub.sap.com? Werden beide in Zukunft zusammengelegt?

Das ist der langfristige Plan. Im laufenden Betrieb kann das leider nur nach und nach umgestellt werden, incl. der Informationen von training.sap.com die auch migriert werden. Ziel ist alles auf learning.sap.com zu haben. Dort wurden z.B. auch schon die opensap-Kurse migriert, Informationen zu Zertifizierungen und weiteres. Dies wird zunehmend der single-point of access, bei dem manche Inhalte oder Features wie Re-Zertifizierung oder Hands-on Systems, Live-Sessions nur über das Learning Hub Abo nutzbar sind.

Weiterführende Ressourcen

Weitere Informationen und Details zu dem Thema findet Ihr unter folgenden Adressen – klickt Euch gerne mal durch:

  • Die Unterlagen zu der Session findet Ihr hier: Aufzeichnung & Folien
  • SAP Learning Hub – die Lernplattform im Überblick
  • Alle SAP-Zertifizierungen in der Übersicht
  • SAP-Zertifizierungsfinder: Finden Sie die richtige Zertifizierung für Ihren Bedarf
  • Schulungen und Zertifizierung von SAP als umfassender Überblick
  • SAP-Zertifizierung auf learning.sap.com: Euer zentraler Einstiegspunkt
  • Partner Competency Framework: Alle Anforderungen für SAP-Partner
  • Bleibt zu Schulungen und Zertifizierungen auf dem Laufenden mit dem SAP Learning Newsletter!

Habt Ihr Fragen? Hinterlasst sie gerne in den Kommentaren!

Hi @thorsten-d,

thanks for the update on the new receiver adapter.
Referring to the documentation I cannot identify whether the configuration supports also property parameters in the query. Can you confirm or deny?

Our GraphQL query to update the externalID of the Fact Sheet looks like this:

{"query":"mutation($id:ID!, $patches: [Patch]!) {
 updateFactSheet(id:$id, patches:$patches) {
 factSheet {
 id
 ...on Interface {
 externalId {
 externalId
 }
 }
 }
 }
 }","variables":{"id":"${property.Id}","patches":[{"op":"replace","path":"/externalId","value":"{"externalId":"${property.interfaceId}"}"}]}}

However, this results into a HTTP 500 error.

Thanks & best regards,
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: HeaderInfo - Transalate or change Text 'Object Information'
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am experiencing the same behavior after switching to OData V4. A new “Header” tab and the “Object Information” section are displayed. How can I remove these?

My Annotations:

@ui: {
    headerInfo: {
        typeName: 'Measure',
        typeNamePlural: 'Measures',
        title: {
            type: #STANDARD, value: 'MeasureTitle'
        },
        description: {
            value: 'MeasureTypeText'
        }
    },
}
  @ui.facet: [
    {
      id: 'Measure',
      type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
      purpose: #STANDARD,
      position: 10
    }
  ]

  @ui: { identification: [{ position: 10 }],
         lineItem: [ { position: 10 } ] }
  MeasureTitle;

  @ui: { identification: [{ position: 20 }],
         lineItem: [ { position: 20 } ],
         textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY }
  MeasureType;

  @ui: { identification: [{ position: 30 }],
         lineItem: [ { position: 30 } ],
         textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY }
  MeasureSubtype;

  @ui: { identification: [{ position: 40 }],
         lineItem: [ { position: 40 } ] }
  MeasureLocation;

 

Best, Manuel

entry.title: Unlock the Power of SAP Universal ID: Simplify Your Digital Identity
entry.content:
First let’s understand what SAP Universal ID is and what are the benefits of having one. 



Are you finding it challenging to manage numerous SAP accounts and passwords? Imagine having a single, unified account that links all your existing company associations, allowing you to switch between them effortlessly. That's where SAP Universal ID comes in! 

 

SAP Universal ID will be your personal SAP identity, the S-user ID is a Business SAP identity given by company administrators and can be utilized as an entitlement under the SAP Universal ID.


With SAP Universal ID, you can: 
 

  • Take control of your experience: Own and manage your personal SAP identity. 

 

  • Keep your account for life: Use it wherever you go, regardless of where you work. 

 

  • Use one login for all your accounts: Manage one account and password by linking all your existing company accounts (S-user IDs) in one universal account.  


Once your account is set up, you can access the SAP Universal ID Account Manager, which is your single-entry point to view, update, and manage your personal information, account security settings, and associations to companies. 

 
Here is a link to the video https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2023/03/d0944279-6a7e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html and see how simple it is to create SAP Universal ID. 

 

SAP Universal ID will also preserve users' community reputations and learning certificates. This ensures the continuity of personal achievements across SAP platforms and services, reinforcing its pivotal role in user identity management within the SAP ecosystem. 

Benefits of having SAP Universal ID: 

  • SAP Universal ID allows you to combine all your existing and future S/P-user accounts under a single password, eliminating the need to keep track of all your S/P-user IDs. 
  • SAP Universal ID includes all your existing and future S/P-user identities. In the SAP Universal ID Account Manager, you can manage all your user identities, personal data, security, and privacy settings 
  • Your SAP Universal ID is personal and belongs to you throughout your working life. You can use it to preserve your learning certificates and community reputation even when you change job. 
  • In general, it is not mandatory to create SAP Universal ID. However, if you want to preserve your community reputations and learning certificates, you will need to have an SAP Universal ID. 

Now it has been made mandate to add a secondary email ID under SAP Universal ID to ensure that your SAP Universal ID is compliant. 

 
Here is the link to add recovery email to your SAP Universal ID - https://learninghub.sap.com/help-center/faq/uidlink and https://learning.sap.com/how-to-assign-a-second-email-account 

 

If you are ever in doubt, please contact the Customer Interaction Center team CIC 

If you wish to unlink a secondary email ID from your primary SAP Universal ID or delete your SAP Universal ID entirely, you can contact CIC via email with the necessary details. A confirmation email, including all your linked email IDs, is required to proceed. This ensures a written record of your request and helps prevent any discrepancies. 
 

Steps to Unlink a Secondary Email ID: 

  • UID (email ID created via account.sap.com) : 
  • E-mail, or S/P IDs to be unlinked: 
  • Reason: 
  • Proof of ownership (The request must be sent from the UID email address, or alternatively from an email account linked to it): 
  • Please confirm if you have any certifications linked to your SAP Universal ID.

If your inquiry pertains to SAP certifications and exams, please contact our SAP Training/Certification team by following the below steps: 

 

  • You would first need to login to https://learning.sap.com
    • Scroll towards the bottom and click on Get Support under learning support. 
    • Again, scroll down towards the bottom look for Create a new case or check the status of an existing one 
    • Once done, you will be asked to enter the details and that should be sent to the training team, additionally you can check the details of yours existing training cases.

      Thanks for reading!    
         
      We would love to hear from you, let us know what you think? 

       If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community    

       Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing - Data Migration
entry.content:
Hi Prasannakrishna,
currently there is no transport mechanism in Subscription Billing to copy master data from one tenant to another.
However, as you already concluded, you can use the APIs to retrieve the business configuration and rate plan templates from one tenant and create them in another tenant. 
Same is possible for rate plans and products, but you need to consider that the IDs of rate plan templates & rate plans will be different in each tenant. 
Best,
Carsten

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP and oData v4 max items (or growing threshold...
entry.content:
Hi, 

i got the same problem and found a solution.

Extend the controller with the guided development.
(The Controller for the Object Page with the Table you want to modify)
Add this code:

⚡Replace the XXXXX with your correct controller name⚡

sap.ui.define([
	"sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension"
], function (ControllerExtension) {
	"use strict";

	return ControllerExtension.extend("XXXXX.ext.controller.Header_Page_Controller_Extension", {


		override: {

			onInit: function () {

				var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
			},

			onAfterRendering: function () {
				var oTable = this._getObjectPageTable();
				if (oTable) {
					oTable.setGrowing(true);
					oTable.setGrowingScrollToLoad(true);
					oTable.setGrowingThreshold(50);
				}
			}
		},

		_getObjectPageTable: function () {

			var oView = this.getView();

			var aTables = oView.findAggregatedObjects(true, function (oControl) {
				return oControl.isA("sap.m.Table");
			});

			return (aTables && aTables.length) ? aTables[0] : null;
		}

	});
});

entry.title: Transferring Master Data from SAP IBP to SAP ECC: Best Integration Methods
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is a powerful tool for supply chain planning, but seamless integration with SAP ECC (or SAP S/4HANA) is crucial for end-to-end business processes. A key challenge for organizations is transferring master data (such as product, location, and BOM data) from SAP IBP to SAP ECC efficiently. In this blog, we will explore the best solutions to achieve this integration.

Why is Master Data Integration Important?

Master data consistency across SAP IBP and SAP ECC ensures that planning and execution processes are aligned. A well-integrated system leads to better decision-making, improved operational efficiency, and reduced data discrepancies.

Solutions for Transferring Master Data from SAP IBP to SAP ECC

SAP provides multiple integration options based on the complexity and frequency of data transfer needs. Below are the key solutions:

  1. SAP Cloud Integration for data services

SAP Cloud Integration for Data Services (SCI/CI-DS) is a middleware solution designed to transfer data between SAP IBP and SAP ECC.

How It Works:

  • Extracts master data from IBP and pushes it to ECC via IDocs, BAPIs, or direct table updates.
  • Supports scheduling and transformation of data before sending it to ECC.
  • Provides pre-built integration templates for standard IBP-to-ECC data flow.

Best For:

✔ Standard IBP-ECC integrations
✔ Scheduled data transfer
✔ Scenarios where transformation of data is needed

  1. SAP IBP Application Jobs (Excel UI + Web UI)

SAP IBP provides an export job feature that allows users to extract master data and upload it to ECC manually or through automation.

How It Works:

  • IBP application jobs export master data in a CSV or XML file.
  • ECC can consume this file using an ABAP program that updates the relevant master data tables.

Best For:

✔ Simple and periodic data transfers
✔ Ad-hoc updates without middleware
✔ Organizations looking for a cost-effective approach

  1. SAP API Management (OData or REST APIs)

SAP provides OData and REST APIs that enable IBP and ECC to communicate in real-time.

How It Works:

  • IBP exposes OData APIs to fetch master data.
  • SAP ECC consumes this data using ABAP programs or middleware (SAP PO, SAP CPI).
  • The data is inserted into ECC via BAPIs or direct table updates.

Best For:

✔ Real-time integration needs
✔ Event-driven updates
✔ Organizations using API-based architecture

  1. SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) with Custom Middleware

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides a flexible way to integrate IBP with ECC.

How It Works:

  • SAP BTP extracts master data from IBP.
  • The data is processed or enriched before being sent to ECC using IDocs or BAPIs.
  • Can be used with SAP HANA Cloud, SAP CPI, or other middleware solutions.

Best For:

✔ Custom integration scenarios
✔ Businesses requiring additional data processing
✔ Hybrid cloud environments

  1. SAP Data Intelligence

SAP Data Intelligence (DI) is a powerful tool for data integration, orchestration, and analytics.

How It Works:

  • Uses AI/ML-based pipelines to extract, transform, and load master data.
  • Can connect SAP IBP, SAP ECC, and third-party applications.
  • Offers advanced data governance and monitoring.

Best For:

✔ Enterprise-wide integration with analytics
✔ Businesses looking for AI-driven data transfers
✔ Scenarios involving multiple data sources

Comparison of Integration Methods

Solution

Best For

Integration Type

SCI (CPI-DS)

Standard IBP-to-ECC integration

Scheduled batch processing

IBP Application Jobs

Simple, ad-hoc exports

File-based transfer

SAP API Management

Real-time data transfer

OData/REST APIs

SAP BTP (Middleware)

Custom integration needs

IDocs, BAPIs, or direct updates

SAP Data Intelligence

Advanced analytics-driven transfers

AI & ML-based pipelines

Conclusion

The choice of integration method depends on factors like real-time vs. batch processing, data transformation needs, and existing system landscape.

  • If real-time updates are required, API-based integration is the best choice.
  • For scheduled updates, SAP CPI-DS or IBP export jobs are ideal.
  • If your organization needs customized or hybrid integration, SAP BTP or SAP Data Intelligence can be explored.

By selecting the right integration approach, businesses can ensure seamless master data synchronization between SAP IBP and SAP ECC, leading to more efficient supply chain operations.

 

What’s Next? If you’re looking to implement an IBP-ECC integration strategy, consider evaluating your business needs, existing infrastructure, and long-term goals.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@ernlin  discussed with @smitajalit  and please find below :

If customer Requirement is :

  • to allow TFA for Employee
  • Onboardee login via IAS Username/Password screen and NOT by TFA.

SAP Response:

TFA can be applied to onboardees, but also RBA rules can also be configured to require TFA for employees while exempting onboardees.

In summary, the SCI administrator has the flexibility to configure required TFA for different set of users ( for example differentiating them based on their types).

Please let us know any further details

Thanks,

Gopi
